title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A classic novel about the Bennet family and the relationship between Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy.

chapters.title: Chapter 1
chapters.summary: The novel opens with the famous line about single men in possession of fortune. Mrs. Bennet is eager to see her daughters married. Mr. Bingley arrives in the neighborhood.

chapters.title: Chapter 2
chapters.summary: Mr. Bennet visits Mr. Bingley, surprising his family. The news of the ball where Bingley will attend spreads excitement.

chapters.title: Chapter 3
chapters.summary: At the ball, Mr. Bingley dances with Jane. Mr. Darcy refuses to be introduced to Elizabeth, calling her 'tolerable, but not handsome enough to tempt me.'

chapters.title: Chapter 4
chapters.summary: Mrs. Bennet discusses the ball with her husband, expressing hope that Jane might marry Bingley.

chapters.title: Chapter 5
chapters.summary: Mrs. Bennet visits Bingley and his sisters. Elizabeth notices Mr. Darcy's pride but also his improved behavior.

chapters.title: Chapter 6
chapters.summary: Elizabeth and Jane discuss their impressions of the evening. Jane likes Bingley, while Elizabeth is critical of Darcy.

chapters.title: Chapter 7
chapters.summary: Jane visits Bingley's house. Mrs. Bennet insists on sending a carriage to ensure Jane stays for dinner.

chapters.title: Chapter 8
chapters.summary: Jane stays for dinner. Elizabeth walks to Netherfield to check on her sister and meets Mr. Bingley and Darcy.

chapters.title: Chapter 9
chapters.summary: Elizabeth walks with the officers. She overhears Mr. Darcy speaking about her, increasing her dislike.

chapters.title: Chapter 10
chapters.summary: Elizabeth is invited to dinner. At the table, she amuses herself by observing the characters of the guests.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: The story of Victor Frankenstein and the creature he brings to life.

chapters.title: Letter 1
chapters.summary: Robert Walton writes to his sister about his Arctic expedition and meets a mysterious man on the ice.

chapters.title: Letter 2
chapters.summary: Walton continues his account, describing how the man reveals his name as Frankenstein and begins his tale.

chapters.title: Chapter 1
chapters.summary: Victor Frankenstein describes his happy childhood in Geneva, his loving family, and his early interest in natural philosophy.

chapters.title: Chapter 2
chapters.summary: Victor's mother dies of scarlet fever. This tragedy deepens his character and drives him toward his scientific pursuits.

chapters.title: Chapter 3
chapters.summary: Victor goes to the University of Ingolstadt where he becomes obsessed with creating life. He discovers the secret of animation.

chapters.title: Chapter 4
chapters.summary: Victor works on his creation for months. On a rainy night, he brings his creature to life but is horrified by its appearance.

chapters.title: Chapter 5
chapters.summary: The creature shows kindness but Victor abandons him. The creature learns to read and reads Paradise Lost.

chapters.title: Chapter 6
chapters.summary: The creature discovers his own appearance and becomes bitter. He observes a family in a cottage and learns about humanity.

chapters.title: Chapter 7
chapters.summary: The creature saves a girl from drowning, but when he approaches humans, they attack him. He becomes vengeful.

chapters.title: Chapter 8
chapters.summary: The creature demands that Victor create a female companion. Victor agrees but destroys the female creation, leading to tragedy.

title: Moby Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: The epic tale of Captain Ahab's obsessive quest to hunt the white whale, Moby Dick.

chapters.title: Loomings
chapters.summary: The narrator, Ishmael, introduces himself and expresses his desire to go to sea. He heads to the Spouter-Inn.

chapters.title: The Carpet-Bag
chapters.summary: Ishmael meets the strange harpooneer Queequeg at the inn. They strike up an unexpected friendship.

chapters.title: The Spouter-Inn
chapters.summary: Ishmael and Queequeg decide to share a bed. In the night, Ishmael mistakes Queequeg for a savage and flees, but returns.

chapters.title: The Counterpane
chapters.summary: Ishmael wakes to find Queequeg's arm around him. They become close friends despite their differences.

chapters.title: Breakfast
chapters.summary: Ishmael and Queequeg have breakfast together. Queequeg shows his generous nature by paying for everything.

chapters.title: The Street
chapters.summary: Ishmael wanders the streets of New Bedford looking for a whaling ship.

chapters.title: The Chapel
chapters.summary: Ishmael visits a chapel built by whalers. He sees the importance of whaling to the community.

chapters.title: The Pulpit
chapters.summary: Ishmael meets the Reverend Mr. Mapple who tells the story of Jonah before the service.

chapters.title: The Sermon
chapters.summary: Mapple delivers a powerful sermon about Jonah and the whale, connecting it to the dangers of whaling.

chapters.title: A Bosom Friend
chapters.summary: Ishmael and Queequeg become inseparable. They decide to ship out together on a whaling voyage.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
description: The story of Pip, an orphan who comes into a great fortune and the expectations that come with it.

chapters.title: Chapter 1
chapters.summary: Pip, a young orphan, encounters an escaped convict in the cemetery. The convict threatens him and demands food.

chapters.title: Chapter 2
chapters.summary: Pip steals food from his sister Mrs. Joe and the blacksmith. He helps the convict escape.

chapters.title: Chapter 3
chapters.summary: The convicts are captured. Pip is brought to Miss Havisham's house, where he meets Estella.

chapters.title: Chapter 4
chapters.summary: Pip returns to Miss Havisham's house regularly. He grows fond of Estella despite her coldness.

chapters.title: Chapter 5
chapters.summary: Pip learns about Miss Havisham's tragic past - jilted at the altar, she lives in her wedding dress among decaying wedding feast.

chapters.title: Chapter 6
chapters.summary: Pip becomes an apprentice to Joe, the blacksmith. He is given money to buy items but gives it to a convict.

chapters.title: Chapter 7
chapters.summary: Pip receives a package containing no money, just some articles. He grows restless about his future.

chapters.title: Chapter 8
chapters.summary: Pip is taken to Satis House by Mr. Jaggers. Miss Havisham asks him to play with Estella.

chapters.title: Chapter 9
chapters.summary: Pip confesses his love for Estella. Jaggers tells him about a mysterious benefactor who will make him a gentleman.

chapters.title: Chapter 10
chapters.summary: Pip leaves for London to become a gentleman. He is excited about his great expectations.

title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: A story about the duality of human nature, told through the mysterious Dr. Jekyll and the evil Mr. Hyde.

chapters.title: Story of the Door
chapters.summary: Mr. Utterson the lawyer investigates the link between his friend Dr. Jekyll and the evil Mr. Hyde.

chapters.title: Search for Jekyll
chapters.summary: Utterson examines Jekyll's will, which leaves everything to Hyde. Strange events unfold.

chapters.title: The Carew Murder Case
chapters.summary: Sir Danvers Carew is murdered by Hyde. The police seek Utterson's help in finding Hyde.

chapters.title: The Carew Murder Case (continued)
chapters.summary: A maid witnesses the murder and identifies Hyde as the culprit. Hyde is seen destroying evidence.

chapters.title: Incident of the Letter
chapters.summary: Jekyll shows Utterson a letter from Hyde. Utterson suspects Jekyll is being blackmailed.

chapters.title: Remarkable Case of Dr. Jekyll
chapters.summary: Jekyll's behavior becomes erratic. He appears happy one moment, troubled the next.

chapters.title: Incident at the Window
chapters.summary: Dr. Lanyon falls ill and tells Utterson of a terrible event that changed his life.

chapters.title: The Last Night
chapters.summary: Mr. Utterson breaks into Jekyll's laboratory and discovers the terrible truth about the transformation.

chapters.title: Dr. Lanyon's Narrative
chapters.summary: Lanyon describes how he witnessed Jekyll transform into Hyde. He dies from the shock.

chapters.title: Henry Jekyll's Full Statement
chapters.summary: Jekyll reveals his experiments with dual nature. He explains how he created a potion to separate good and evil.
